epigraph.quote: I shall be sad to leave Mull tomorrow.

per_stop.quote: in fields with drystone walls. Good wine at supper.
A gorgeous white music room. Wall hangings, one embroidered, others printed.
the caretaker was extremely rude just before we went to bed + there was a thunderstorm.
We stopped to see St. Conan's Kirk, light. Carvings.
Oban is like Barmouth, but bigger.
Odd white plastic tents for wash rooms, "carpeted cottage-style tents", washing machine room, reception & drying tents.
other house colours on sea front row: yellow, blue, black, orange, purple, lemon, darker blue, pink, rust.
Glorious sunshine. Even Simon & Eve swam in the loch when we arrived.
We had lunch looking across at loch at Glencoe.
The country side has become more wooded. We have reached the Trossachs.
interesting displays by stone-masons, wood carvers, embroiderers. An interesting Great Kitchen.
a knight in armour & his wife. Well worth seeing.
It is a large field with a beautiful view of the Firth of the Fourth.
Saw the eclipse through a pin hole in a cereal packet, on the back table of the van after drawing all the curtains.
Purdie & Finnian came out to join us & had crisps & ½ an eccles cake. They are absolutely gorgeous & well-behaved.
climbing on the rocks, collecting cowrie shells & enjoying the beach.
The view was one of the best I've ever seen, the hills to the west of Douro, past Edinburgh & to beyond North Berwick.
the wool mill town, we stopped & bought a brown cashmere sweater.
